vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-709: self-improving flywheel — retro-agent, skill-change pipeline, audience-scoped skills, Q&A detection, autonomous daemon

plan.title: feat(flywheel): self-improving flywheel — retro agent, skill-change pipeline, audience-scoped skills, Q&A detection, autonomous daemon
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Panopticon's long-term value depends on skill quality, but the learning loop is manual and lossy — retros don't exist, skill refinement is episodic, skill audiences are undifferentiated, and inline skill edits raise approval prompts that halt the flywheel. The compounding mechanism never engages.
narratives.Proposal: Ship an end-to-end self-improvement system as one epic: retro-agent fires on merge, synthesis turns retros into PAN issues with a 3-signal threshold, skill changes flow through the normal pipeline with narrow work-agent scope, pan sync respects audience, Q&A detection marks waiting agents, and an autonomous Cloister daemon drives the loop. Assumes PAN-705 (command taxonomy reorg) is merged — this plan writes against the post-PAN-705 surface.

items.title: Verify PAN-705 post-reorg surface exists before starting

narrative.Action: First bead the work agent runs. Confirm PAN-705 has merged and the post-reorg CLI/API/skill surfaces exist. If not, halt and escalate — do not plan against the legacy surface.

subItems.title: `src/cli/commands/work/` directory does not exist (collapsed by PAN-705)
`pan done`, `pan approve`, `pan issue`, `pan show`, `pan status`, `pan sync` are callable top-level verbs
`src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts` is the current shape (no `work.ts`)
`pan sync` already performs clean-slate delete of legacy skill copies in `~/.claude/skills/`
If any of the above are missing, halt work and open a tmux tell to the operator; do not proceed to bead schema-audience

narrative.Action: Create `packages/contracts/src/skills.ts` declaring `SkillFrontmatter` with a required `audience: 'operator' | 'agent' | 'both'` field and a read-time default of `operator` for backward compat. Export a parser/validator used by pan sync, review-agent, and skill-lint.

subItems.title: `packages/contracts/src/skills.ts` exports `SkillFrontmatter` type with audience enum
Parser returns `audience: 'operator'` when the field is missing from an existing skill file (backward compat)
Parser rejects an invalid audience value (not operator/agent/both) with a clear error
Unit tests cover: valid frontmatter, missing field, invalid value, plus a real existing skill from `skills/` parses cleanly
`packages/contracts` is rebuilt (`cd packages/contracts && npm run build`) so consumers see the new type

narrative.Action: Extend the `SpecialistType` union in `src/lib/cloister/specialists.ts` with `'retro-agent'`, add its metadata entry (displayName, description, model: `sonnet`, spawn-run-exit lifecycle flag), and wire `src/lib/cloister/prompts.ts` to load `prompts/retro-agent.md`.

subItems.title: `SpecialistType` union includes `'retro-agent'`
Specialist metadata entry declares model `claude-sonnet-4-6`, autoWake false, spawn-run-exit true
`renderPrompt('retro-agent')` resolves to `src/lib/cloister/prompts/retro-agent.md` and returns its contents
Existing specialists (review, merge, test, inspect, uat) still resolve their prompts unchanged (no regression)

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/cloister/prompts/retro-agent.md` with the surprise-centered framing from PRD section 1. Document the bounded input set, the output schema (YAML frontmatter + markdown body), the no-op escape hatch, and the hard instruction to self-validate schema before exiting.

subItems.title: Prompt instructs the agent to write `no-op` and stop if nothing surprised it (boring is good)
Prompt declares bounded inputs verbatim: STATE.md, vBRIEF, feedback/*.md, tmux tails (last 200 lines), FLYWHEEL-STATE row, gh pr view --comments, merge commit + branch commits
Prompt specifies output location `docs/flywheel/retros/<issue-id>-<timestamp>.md` and the full YAML+markdown schema from PRD section 1
Prompt requires at least ONE `proposed_changes` entry or an explicit `no_op` reason — no narrative-only retros
Prompt caps output at ~500 words and requires self-validation of schema before exiting

narrative.Action: Create `skills/retro-workflow/SKILL.md` with `audience: agent`, trigger-context description, and the checklist the retro-agent follows: read inputs in order, apply surprise filter, write output, validate schema, exit.

subItems.title: File exists with valid frontmatter including `audience: agent`
Description follows agent convention: `"Triggered when <condition>. Use to <action>."` (not the CLI-verb operator convention)
Checklist body references the input order and schema from the retro-agent prompt (single source of truth — skill links back to prompt file)

narrative.Action: Implement the input-collection helper used by retro-agent at spawn time: reads STATE.md, plan.vbrief.json, every feedback/*.md, last 200 lines of each agent tmux history under `~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/`, the issue's row in `docs/FLYWHEEL-STATE.md`, `gh pr view --comments`, merge commit + branch commit list. Packages them as a single bounded context the prompt references.

subItems.title: Helper at `src/lib/flywheel/retro-inputs.ts` (new) takes an `issueId` and returns a structured input bundle
Tmux tail reads are capped at 200 lines each and gracefully handle missing session histories
FLYWHEEL-STATE row extraction returns `null` cleanly when the issue has no row (e.g., it never entered the pipeline visibly)
All file I/O uses `fs/promises` (CLAUDE.md blocking-call rule)
Unit test: given a fixture issue with STATE.md + vBRIEF + 2 feedback files, bundle returns all three in the expected structure

narrative.Action: Implement `src/lib/cloister/retro-agent.ts` with `spawnRetroAgent(issueId)`. Spawns an ephemeral tmux session under `retro-<issueId>`, launches Claude Code with the retro-agent prompt + bundled inputs, enforces a 5-minute hard cap (SIGKILL via deacon), and exits without leaving the session around. Does NOT reuse the long-lived specialist tmux pattern.

subItems.title: `spawnRetroAgent(issueId)` returns a promise that resolves after retro completes or the 5-min cap fires
Session is killed and removed (`tmux kill-session`) after exit — no orphaned retro sessions
5-minute cap is enforced by deacon calling `SIGKILL` on the session if not finished; deacon logs the overrun
Spawn uses `sendKeysAsync` (load-buffer/paste-buffer pattern) for any text delivery to the session, per CLAUDE.md tmux rule
Smoke test: spawn against a fixture issue writes a valid retro markdown file and returns within the cap

narrative.Action: Create `docs/flywheel/retros/` directory convention and a writer helper that validates the retro against the schema before writing (and rejects invalid output from the retro-agent by failing the spawn). File name: `<issue-id>-<unix-ts>.md`.

subItems.title: Writer helper validates frontmatter (issue, agent, run, cycle_count, friction_score 0-10, surprise bool, proposed_changes array OR no_op)
Writer uses `fs/promises.writeFile` and creates parent dirs as needed
Writer rejects a retro missing both `proposed_changes` and `no_op` (no narrative-only retros)
Unit tests cover: valid retro, missing proposed_changes, invalid friction_score, wrong surprise type

narrative.Action: Extend `src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts::postMergeLifecycle()` (or the nearest `onMergeComplete` equivalent) to call `spawnRetroAgent(issueId)` after post-merge cleanup succeeds. Retro failures must NOT roll back the merge — log and continue. Respects postMergeLifecycle idempotency guards (PAN-328).

subItems.title: On successful merge + post-merge cleanup, `spawnRetroAgent(issueId)` is invoked
Retro failure is caught and logged; merge remains completed (no rollback, no stuck state)
The existing postMergeLifecycle idempotency guard (PAN-328) is untouched and still prevents the specialists/done → onMergeComplete → postMergeLifecycle loop
Retro spawn happens AFTER the existing postMergeLifecycle Docker cleanup step 6 (never removes that step)

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/flywheel/synthesis.ts::runSynthesis()`. Reads every non-archived retro, filters to `surprise: true`, groups by signature (same target skill + same gap description + same audience), applies the 3-signal threshold. Above-threshold groups become issue-proposal objects; below-threshold become watchlist entries.

subItems.title: Reads only retros under `docs/flywheel/retros/` excluding `archive/`
Grouping key is deterministic: `(target_skill, audience, normalized_gap_description)`
Threshold: groups with `retro_count >= 3` become issue proposals; smaller groups become watchlist entries
Returns a typed result: `{ proposals: IssueProposal[], watchlist: WatchlistEntry[], processedRetros: string[], filterRatio: number }`
All file I/O uses `fs/promises`
Unit tests: 1 retro (watchlist), 2 retros same signature (watchlist), 3 retros same signature (proposal), 3 retros different signatures (3 separate watchlist entries, no proposal)

narrative.Action: Create the two new GitHub labels (`flywheel-change`, `substrate-improvement`) via `gh label create`. Document the creation in `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` (or nearest equivalent) so a fresh clone can reproduce them.

subItems.title: `gh label list` on the repo shows both `flywheel-change` and `substrate-improvement` labels
Labels have distinct colors and short descriptions
A setup/docs note references the labels so they can be recreated if wiped

items.title: Synthesis files PAN issues for above-threshold proposals

narrative.Action: Extend synthesis to file PAN issues via the tracker client for each above-threshold proposal. Label `flywheel-change`, title `flywheel: <verb> <skill-name> — <summary>`, body contains the proposal + links to every triggering retro + the proposed patch as a code block. Cap at 10 issues per run to prevent storms; overflow rolls to the next cycle.

subItems.title: Each proposal maps to exactly one GitHub issue with the `flywheel-change` label applied
Issue body includes triggering retro filenames (or links), the aggregated signal count, and the proposed SKILL.md patch as a fenced code block
If `proposals.length > 10`, only the top 10 by aggregated friction score are filed; the remainder logs as deferred
Priority is the median friction score across triggering retros, clamped to the repo's priority enum
Runs via the existing tracker client (not a new gh wrapper) to inherit retry/auth behavior

items.title: Append new section to `docs/FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md` per run

narrative.Action: Create `docs/FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md` if absent (with header), and append one run section per synthesis invocation matching the PRD section 4 schema. Append-only — never rewrites existing sections.

subItems.title: File is created on first run with the header + intro line (no cost content)

Each run appends one section with: run N, timestamp, trigger (`/all-up` or `daemon event`), quick stats, skill-change issues filed (linked), substrate bugs fixed inline, top friction patterns, watchlist, wins
Writer NEVER touches content above the new section (append-only semantics)
No cost field, no spend chart, no dollar amounts anywhere in the report (confirms cost-out decision)

narrative.Action: After synthesis files issues and writes FLYWHEEL-REPORT, move each processed retro to `docs/flywheel/retros/archive/run-N/<filename>`. Watchlist retros stay in the main directory. After 30 days a watchlist retro without new signals moves to archive with a `wontfix` marker.

subItems.title: Processed retros are moved to `archive/run-N/` (not copied — originals removed)
Run number N is derived from the FLYWHEEL-REPORT section count (or persistent counter)
Watchlist retros older than 30 days without new matching signals are archived with a `wontfix` reason appended to frontmatter

items.title: Synthesis commit + push archive moves + FLYWHEEL-REPORT update

narrative.Action: After synthesis finishes (issues filed, report appended, retros archived), commit the diff and push to main so the next cycle sees the clean state. Commit message follows the repo convention.

subItems.title: Single commit contains: FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md append + archive moves + any watchlist bookkeeping
Commit message: `flywheel: run N — <count> issues filed, <count> watchlisted`
Push is skipped (with log) if the working tree is unchanged — synthesis is a no-op safely
After the daemon cycle completes, `git status` on `main` is clean (enforces AC-14)

items.title: Add Step 8 (synthesis) + no-inline rule to `all-up` skill

narrative.Action: Edit `skills/all-up/SKILL.md` to insert Step 8 (synthesis) between Step 7 (main hygiene) and Step 7.5 (FLYWHEEL-STATE update). Also add the explicit rule: 'skill changes are never inline edits during /all-up — always file via synthesis.' Document the `docs/flywheel/retros/` directory and the FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md schema briefly.

subItems.title: Step 8 section exists after Step 7 and before Step 7.5 and describes calling `runSynthesis()`
An explicit rule block states skill changes are never inline edits during `/all-up`
An explicit note says retro-agent fires automatically on merge — don't run retros manually during /all-up
Substrate-bug tiering rule: blocker-tier can be inline; non-blocker becomes a `substrate-improvement` issue

items.title: Update repo `CLAUDE.md` with skill-change pipeline rule

narrative.Action: Append a new section to the repo-level `CLAUDE.md` explaining why skills are edited through the flywheel pipeline, not directly. Includes the two escape hatches (file a `flywheel-change` issue OR write a retro note).

subItems.title: New section named `## Skill changes go through the flywheel pipeline` exists
Section names both escape hatches: filing a `flywheel-change` PAN issue or writing a retro note

items.title: Work agent behavior branch for `flywheel-change` label

narrative.Action: Teach the work agent (and the planning entry gate) that `flywheel-change` issues: (1) skip planning — the issue body IS the plan; (2) narrow scope to `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` only; (3) reject any diff touching non-skill files back to synthesis with a `mis-scoped` marker; (4) never create beads for skill changes (they are atomic).

subItems.title: Starting a work agent on a `flywheel-change` issue skips the `pan plan` phase and uses the issue body as the plan
Work agent refuses to commit a diff that touches any file outside `skills/` when the label is set, and marks the issue `mis-scoped`
`pan plan` on a `flywheel-change` issue auto-finalizes with a single bead containing the issue body as the plan (no interactive planning)
Inspect agent adapts to the narrow-scope case (single-file SKILL.md) without regressing verification on normal issues

narrative.Action: New module exporting `lintSkill(path)` that validates frontmatter schema (via the contracts module), absence of broken skill references, markdown syntax, and 'focused diff' coherence (not a shotgun rewrite). Shared between review-agent, test-agent, and `pan admin skills audit`.

subItems.title: `lintSkill(path)` returns `{ valid: boolean, errors: LintError[] }`
Rejects skills missing the `audience` field once the schema is enforced (with a grace default of `operator` at read time — the lint distinguishes)
Rejects skills referencing a non-existent skill name in description or body
Unit tests cover valid, invalid frontmatter, broken ref, and a real skill from `skills/`

items.title: Review agent runs skill-lint on `flywheel-change` issues

narrative.Action: Extend the review-agent prompt or orchestration so that on `flywheel-change` issues it runs `lintSkill(path)` for every modified skill file and blocks approval on any lint error. No bandaid — the review-agent is the gate.

subItems.title: Review-agent prompt (`src/lib/cloister/prompts/review.md`) instructs running the lint for flywheel-change issues
Review fails (feedback written) if any modified SKILL.md fails lint
Review passes cleanly on a valid single-file SKILL.md change

items.title: Test agent runs `pan sync --dry-run` for flywheel-change issues

narrative.Action: Extend test-agent so that on `flywheel-change` issues it runs `pan sync --dry-run` and validates the resulting sync layout is valid (no broken symlinks, audience routing produces the expected destinations).

subItems.title: Test-agent prompt instructs running `pan sync --dry-run` on flywheel-change issues
Dry-run failure blocks the test phase with explicit feedback
Dry-run success on the sample skill-change fixture lets the test phase pass

narrative.Action: Extend `src/lib/sync.ts` (and `src/cli/commands/sync.ts`) to read each skill's `audience` and route: `operator` → `~/.claude/skills/<name>/`, `agent` → referenced in workspace CLAUDE.md (not copied to `~/.claude/skills/`), `both` → both. Layer on top of PAN-705's clean-slate delete (do not conflict).

subItems.title: Operator-audience skills land in `~/.claude/skills/<name>/` as today
Agent-audience skills are NOT copied to `~/.claude/skills/` (they're missing from that destination after sync)
`both` audience skills land in both `~/.claude/skills/` and the workspace CLAUDE.md reference section
Skills missing the `audience` field fall back to operator routing (backward compat)
PAN-705's clean-slate delete behavior is preserved (not reverted or duplicated)
`pan sync --dry-run` shows per-skill destinations grouped by audience

items.title: Workspace create injects `## Available Skills (agent audience)` into CLAUDE.md

narrative.Action: Extend the workspace bootstrap (inside `pan workspace create` or its library equivalent) to append a `## Available Skills (agent audience)` section to the workspace CLAUDE.md listing every agent-audience skill with name, description, and a link back to the SKILL.md in the repo.

subItems.title: Fresh workspace CLAUDE.md contains the agent-audience skills section
Each listed skill links to its SKILL.md in the repo (relative path works from inside the worktree)
Existing workspace CLAUDE.md content (project instructions) is preserved — section is appended, not overwritten

narrative.Action: Add the `pan admin skills audit` subcommand under the post-PAN-705 admin namespace. Walks every `skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, reports each with audience + sync destination + missing-field flag. `--fix` cleans up stale copies.

subItems.title: `pan admin skills audit` runs cleanly and produces a table: skill, audience, sync destination, status
Missing-field skills are listed with `audience: (missing → operator default)` and flagged as warnings
`--fix` removes stale copies from `~/.claude/skills/` for skills now declared `agent`
Command uses `fs/promises`, not sync fs calls (CLAUDE.md blocking rule — even though CLI-only, for consistency)

items.title: Backfill `audience` field on every existing skill

narrative.Action: Walk every `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` and add an explicit `audience` value. Classification uses the PRD alignment section: skills matching CLI verbs → `operator`; workflow/pattern skills used by autonomous agents → `agent`; skills both sides invoke → `both`. Commit one-by-one or in logical batches for a reviewable diff.

subItems.title: Every skill in `skills/` has an explicit `audience:` line in its frontmatter
`pan admin skills audit` reports zero skills with missing audience field
Classification rationale is documented inline or in the PR description (why each was operator/agent/both)
Before starting the backfill the work agent verifies no open PRs touch `skills/` (to avoid merge conflicts)
After backfill, `pan sync --dry-run` produces a sync layout that matches the pre-backfill operator-only layout for skills classified as `operator` (no regression)

items.title: Add `waiting-on-human` agent state to runtime schema

narrative.Action: Extend `AgentRuntimeState` / the runtime.json schema with a `waiting-on-human` state plus optional `waitingReason` field. All consumers of agent state must handle the new value.

subItems.title: `src/lib/agents.ts` types declare `waiting-on-human` as a valid state
`saveAgentRuntimeState()` accepts the new state without error
Every call site reading the state handles `waiting-on-human` (grep + inspect in tests)

narrative.Action: Extend `~/.panopticon/bin/pre-tool-hook` (bash) to detect when Claude Code is in an approval-waiting state and write `state: waiting-on-human` + reason into `runtime.json`. Investigate the Claude Code `Notification` hook event as a more direct signal (fires on approval-needed); wire it if viable. Also determine whether pre-tool-hook can serve PostToolUse (clearing the marker) or whether a new binary is needed — document the decision.

subItems.title: Hook writes `state: waiting-on-human` to runtime.json when an approval-required signal is detected
Hook clears the marker (returns to `state: active`) after approval resolves — either by reusing pre-tool-hook for PostToolUse or by adding a dedicated clear-hook binary, whichever is cleanest

`~/.claude/settings.json` is updated to wire the hook(s) to the correct Claude Code events, without removing existing hooks (pre-tool-hook, heartbeat-hook, tldr-read-enforcer, pre-compact-hook, post-compact-hook)
The investigation into using the Claude Code `Notification` hook event is documented (either wired or with a clear rationale for not using it)
Manual test: triggering an approval prompt in a real Claude Code session marks the agent `waiting-on-human`; accepting it clears the marker

narrative.Action: Teach `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts` to NOT count stuck-timer ticks against agents in `waiting-on-human` state under the normal threshold. Use a 4x longer threshold before even considering recovery, so genuinely stuck agents still get unblocked.

subItems.title: Deacon's stuck-check skips force-kill when `state === 'waiting-on-human'` until 4x the normal stuck threshold
Deacon emits a distinct event type (`agent-waiting`) so the dashboard can surface the state without duplicating stuck-alert logic
Existing stuck-recovery paths unchanged for agents in `active`/`running` states (no regression)

narrative.Action: Planning agents (this planner's successor) are paused during discovery Q&A. Write `state: waiting-on-human` + `waitingReason: 'planning Q&A'` to the session's runtime.json during AskUserQuestion so the dashboard badge lights up. Clear on answer received.

subItems.title: During an AskUserQuestion call the planning session's runtime.json shows `state: waiting-on-human`
After user answers, state returns to `active`
Mechanism works without depending on Claude Code's internal hook — write the marker from the launcher/wrapper that manages the planning session

narrative.Action: Add a yellow clock badge with text 'Awaiting your input' to the agent card and kanban issue card when the agent state is `waiting-on-human`. Clicking the badge opens the terminal panel for that agent scrolled to the bottom.

subItems.title: Agent card shows the badge when the backing runtime.json state is `waiting-on-human`
Kanban issue card shows the same badge for issues whose active agent is waiting
Clicking the badge navigates to the XTerminal panel for that agent, scrolled to the bottom where the prompt lives
Badge color and iconography are consistent with the existing dashboard status palette

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts` (Effect HTTP style matching the other routes) and expose: `GET /api/flywheel/retros`, `GET /api/flywheel/retros/:issueId`, `GET /api/flywheel/report`, `GET /api/flywheel/daemon/status`. All handlers use `fs/promises` (CLAUDE.md blocking-call rule). Register the route module in the server bootstrap.

subItems.title: `GET /api/flywheel/retros` returns a list of non-archived retros with frontmatter summary
`GET /api/flywheel/retros/:issueId` returns the full markdown body + frontmatter
`GET /api/flywheel/report` returns the FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md content (raw or parsed, caller's choice)
`GET /api/flywheel/daemon/status` returns the daemon state for the dashboard banner
ZERO sync fs calls in these handlers (`readFileSync`, `readdirSync`, etc.) — verified by grep + lint
Route module registered in the Effect HTTP server layer composition

narrative.Action: Add `GET /api/admin/skills/audit` under the post-PAN-705 `/api/admin/*` namespace. Returns the output of the skill audit as JSON for the dashboard to render.

subItems.title: Route returns an array of `{ name, audience, syncDestinations, missingField, stale }` records
Handler uses `fs/promises` and the shared skill-lint module (no duplication)

narrative.Action: Add a new `FlywheelChangesTab.tsx` component rendered as a tab on `AwaitingMergePage.tsx`. Filters issues by `flywheel-change` label. Each card: skill name, diff view (before/after), retro provenance (collapsible), aggregated signal count, merge button, rollback preview link.

subItems.title: AwaitingMergePage has a tab strip (All / Flywheel Changes) — `All` preserves current behavior for non-flywheel issues
Flywheel Changes tab lists only issues with the `flywheel-change` label
Each card renders a diff view for the modified SKILL.md (before → after)
Retro provenance is collapsible; clicking expands shows each triggering retro
Merge button reuses the existing merge flow and succeeds end-to-end on a sample flywheel-change issue

narrative.Action: Add a top-level `/flywheel` route that renders the same tab content plus a metrics panel at the top. Metrics: skills added (week/month/all-time), skills refined, retros processed, retros dropped by no-op filter, top 5 patterns that triggered changes. NO cost metric anywhere.

subItems.title: `/flywheel` route is reachable from the dashboard nav
Metrics panel shows the five metric tiles with real data from the API
ZERO cost/spend/dollar display anywhere on the page (confirms cost-out decision)
UI verified in a browser before calling this bead done (per CLAUDE.md UI testing rule)

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/cloister/flywheel-daemon.ts` with `startFlywheelDaemon()` and `stopFlywheelDaemon()`, mirroring the deacon loop shape. Register it in `CloisterService.start()` after `startDeacon()`. For this bead, the daemon's `tick()` is a no-op that logs its heartbeat — triggers land in subsequent beads.

subItems.title: `startFlywheelDaemon()` is called from `CloisterService.start()` after `startDeacon()`
`stopFlywheelDaemon()` cleanly halts the loop and is called from `CloisterService.stop()`
Loop heartbeat logs visible in cloister startup output
Unit test: scaffold starts and stops without errors and without leaking intervals

narrative.Action: Wire the daemon to two event sources: merge-complete events (spawn retro-agent for the merged issue) and cycle-detected events from FLYWHEEL-STATE (file a substrate-improvement issue for non-blocker cycles; only inline-fix for blocker-tier cycles stopping issues stuck in the CURRENT run).

subItems.title: Daemon subscribes to merge-complete events and calls `spawnRetroAgent` per event
Daemon reads FLYWHEEL-STATE `cycling alerts` section and files `substrate-improvement` issues for non-blocker cycles
Blocker-tier cycles (issues currently stuck in this run) route to inline diagnose, bounded — no code edits by the daemon itself; it files a substrate issue with the blocker label

narrative.Action: Add two scheduled tick paths: every 30 minutes re-read FLYWHEEL-STATE and run synthesis if any new retros exist; every 24 hours run a full flywheel cycle (inventory → diagnose → file → synthesize → report).

subItems.title: 30-min tick calls `runSynthesis()` only if there are new retros since the last run
24h tick runs the full cycle and logs start/end events
Tick intervals are configurable via `~/.panopticon/config.yaml` keys `flywheel.trigger_interval_minutes` and `flywheel.full_cycle_interval_hours`

items.title: Daemon guards: active-session backoff, quiet hours, mutex

narrative.Action: Enforce: (1) back off when a Claude Code session touched a file under `~/.claude/projects/` in the last 10 min; (2) back off during quiet hours from config; (3) single-cycle mutex via `~/.panopticon/flywheel.lock`; (4) never interrupt agents in `waiting-on-human`.

subItems.title: Active-session check examines recent mtime and backs off if threshold crossed
Quiet hours from `flywheel.quiet_hours` are respected (no tick runs during the window)
Mutex on `~/.panopticon/flywheel.lock` prevents concurrent cycles; second attempt logs and exits
Daemon never targets agents in `waiting-on-human` state for any action

items.title: Daemon: Awaiting Merge threshold notify banner

narrative.Action: When the Awaiting Merge queue length exceeds `flywheel.awaiting_merge_notify_threshold`, the daemon writes a dashboard banner event via the existing event store so the UI can show 'N items waiting for merge'.

subItems.title: Threshold check runs on each scheduled tick
Banner event is emitted only when crossing the threshold (not spammed every tick)
Banner clears when the queue drops back below threshold

narrative.Action: Extend the config schema + setup wizard to support a new top-level `flywheel:` section with `autonomous`, `quiet_hours`, `trigger_interval_minutes`, `full_cycle_interval_hours`, `backoff_on_active_session`, `awaiting_merge_notify_threshold`. Provide sensible defaults.

subItems.title: Config schema accepts the new section and returns validated values

Missing section falls back to defaults: `autonomous: true`, `quiet_hours: 22:00-08:00`, `trigger_interval_minutes: 30`, `full_cycle_interval_hours: 24`, `backoff_on_active_session: true`, `awaiting_merge_notify_threshold: 5`
`pan config` / setup wizard surfaces the new knobs

narrative.Action: Add a new `flywheel.mdx` at the Mintlify content root that renders `docs/FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md` (via import/transform or explicit embed — match the pattern of existing .mdx files in this repo). Add a nav entry under 'How It Works → The Flywheel' in `docs.json`. Verify the Mintlify site builds locally.

subItems.title: `flywheel.mdx` exists and renders content from `docs/FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md`
`docs.json` includes a nav entry linking to `/flywheel` under a 'How It Works' group
Mintlify dev build succeeds locally (no broken links, no missing frontmatter)
Page displays the full timeline from FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md (even if FLYWHEEL-REPORT.md is short at first)
